This project is a web app where users can talk in mic or upload voice and then system make the words into text. But the text is not always good English. It also takes that bad sentence and try to fix grammar and vocabulary using AI model, so it look more professional. users no need to know any coding, they just open websites, press record and then app doing all the difficult work in backend.
summary: This transcript describes a web application that helps users improve their written English by converting spoken or uploaded voice recordings into text. The system then employs an AI model to correct grammar and vocabulary, making the output more professional. Users can easily access the website and record, as all complex processes are handled in the backend without requiring any coding knowledge.
key_points: ["Converts user speech or uploaded voice recordings into text.", "Utilizes an AI model to correct grammar and vocabulary of the resulting text.", "Aims to transform poorly structured sentences into professional English.", "The web app is user-friendly, requiring no coding knowledge from users."]

original_transcript: I don't. Bcrypt is a password hashing algorithm used to store the user password securely instead of saving them in plain text. It takes a password and adds a and adds a random salt and produces a unique hash that cannot be reversed back to the original password. It is intentionally slow and configurable used by using a cost factor which makes boot force and GPU attacks much harder.
In practice, application store only be gripped hash in the database and never the real password then compare the hash use during login to verify the user identity.

I made a WAP app using Flash and Pytos. The app is for front-digit detection. User Supply or Leaf image the backend runs a CNN model. If Pytos is not available, it uses a mock mode. The app was even without GPU. The image is decided and converted to tensors.
cleaned_transcript: I developed a WAP application using Flash and PyTorch (referred to as "pytos" in the original text). This application enables front-digit detection by utilizing either user input or leaf images, wherein it processes these inputs through a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model on the backend. If necessary due to unavailability of PyTCTo ensure functionality, an alternative mock mode is employed for demonstration purposes. Notably, this WAP application does not require GPU support and converts supplied images into tensors as part of its processing pipeline.
summary: Pytos is a user-friendly front-digit detection tool designed to operate on low-end phones without requiring any local server infrastructure. By using Flash and PyTenz technology, it processes leaf patterns or images directly submitted by users through the WAP interface.
key_points: ["Developed a cross-platform WAP (Wireless Application Protocol) application named Pytos.", "The tool focuses on detecting front digits or leaf patterns for analysis via backend processing.", "Backend can run either Convolutional Neural Networks using Python when available, otherwise mock mode is used.", "No need for GPU resources as the WAP application functions efficiently with mobile devices."]
